dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9286 IH: Broadband Deployment and Economic Impact Study Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. McClain Delaney (for herself, Mr. Thompson of Mississippi, Mr. Beyer, Mr. Carter of Louisiana, Mr. Fields, Mr. Figures, and Mr. Clyburn) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To direct the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information to conduct a study and submit to Congress a report on the technologies used to provide broadband internet access service, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Broadband Deployment and Economic Impact Study Act of 2026.

section.header: Study and report on broadband internet access service technologies

paragraph.text: The Assistant Secretary shall conduct a study to determine, with respect to each technology used to provide broadband internet access service, the following:

subparagraph.text: Latency, speed (including download and upload speeds), and capacity.
The anticipated lifespan of such technology and the equipment and infrastructure associated with such technology.
The cost to consumers of a monthly subscription and required equipment.

paragraph.text: The technologies covered by the study required by paragraph (1) shall include the following:

subsection.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Assistant Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the results of the study conducted under subsection (a).

paragraph.text: The term Assistant Secretary means the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information.
The term broadband internet access service has the meaning given such term in section 8.1(b) of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation).
